content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
George Holiday 
11402 Downey Avenue 
Downey, 
CA 
90241 
Dear Mr. Holiday: 
october 19, 1987 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-238 
You have requested advice concerning your disclosure 
requirements under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") . .!! 
QUESTION 
Are you required to disclose investments or interests in real 
property which you held prior to the date when the project area 
committee, on which you serve as chair, was added to the city of 
Downeyis conflict of interest code? 
CONCLUSION 
Members of project area committees must disclose reportable 
investments and interests in real property which are held on the 
date they become designated in their city's conflict of interest 
code. 
FACTS 
You are the chair of the city of Downey's Redevelopment 
Project Area Committee ("PAC"). 
The city revised its conflict of 
interest code on July 28, 1987, to require that members of the 
Redevelopment PAC file statements of economic interests ("SEI"). 
Downey Redevelopment PAC members were not required to file SEls 
previously . 
.!!Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et seg. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322,5660 


George Holiday 
October 19, 1987 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Commission has ruled recently that members of PACs must be 
included as designated employees in a city's conflict of interest 
code. 
(In re Rotman (1987) 10 FPPC Ops. 1, copy enclosed.) 
The 
City of Downey revised its conflict of interest code on July 28, 
1987, to require members of the Redevelopment PAC to file SEIs. 
The Commission made it clear in Rotman that: 
" . .. those who were 
not designated in a code, have not been subject to the 
requirements to file disclosure statements, need not file 
statements for past years." 
(In re Rotman, Supra at p. 9.) 
You should complete an "initial" statement for designated 
employees, Form 730. 
Such a statement requires disclosure of your 
reportable investments and real property interests held on the 
effective date of the amended conflict of interest code adopted by 
the City of Downey. 
If you have any questions regarding the advice in this letter, 
please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5662. 
DMG:BWR:kmt 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
6u~~~/c J k~/~A~ 
By: 
Bruce W. Robeck 
Political Reform Consultant 




California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
George Holiday 
11402 Downey Avenue 
Downey, CA 90241 
Dear Mr. Holiday: 
September 21, 1987 
Re: 
87-238 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on September 18, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
"We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. ~dm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP: j aj 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660

CONCLUSION: Members of project area committees must disclose reportable
